g.text: Comparison: ReadIndex vs No-Op Log Entry
- [ ] Leader must check 'rf.state == Leader' after recording readIndex.
- [ ] Quorum check MUST involve a fresh heartbeat exchange.
- [ ] State machine must signal via sync.Cond when lastApplied is incremented.
- [ ] If leadership is lost during wait, client must be redirected.

text.tspan: - [ ] Leader must check 'rf.state == Leader' after recording readIndex.
- [ ] Quorum check MUST involve a fresh heartbeat exchange.
- [ ] State machine must signal via sync.Cond when lastApplied is incremented.
- [ ] If leadership is lost during wait, client must be redirected.

for the same or higher term

g.text: 1. Cluster [A, B, C, D, E] with Leader A.
3. [B, C, D, E] elects B as Leader for Term+1.
5. Client sends read {x} to A (unaware of partition).
6. A (deposed) returns old value {x: 5} because it thinks it is still leader.

text.tspan: 1. Cluster [A, B, C, D, E] with Leader A.
3. [B, C, D, E] elects B as Leader for Term+1.
5. Client sends read {x} to A (unaware of partition).
6. A (deposed) returns old value {x: 5} because it thinks it is still leader.

div.p: ReadIndex is standard in production Raft (Etcd, TiKV) because it avoids the heavy cost of disk I/O for read-only ops.
